rulings.Case Title: 200 KANSAS OWNER LLC VS. KEENWAWA, INC. DBA BRIGHTLOOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appellate Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER 200 KANSAS OWNER LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appellate Attorney'S Fees And Costs is continued to January 11, 2024 to be heard with other pending motion(s). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARLENE RAMOS ET AL VS. JAY POINDEXTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Due To Attorney'S Mistake
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF MARLENE RAMOS, JOSE RAMOS Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Due To Attorney'S Mistake is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the propositions that (1) CCP 473(b) is a "new law" within the meaning of CCP 1008; or that (2) CCP 473(b) is applicable under the procedural posture of this action. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: PALLADIAN WEST, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION FKA VS. ESPERANZA AHUMADA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 4, 2024 line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT PALLADIAN WEST, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION FKA DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The validity of the agent's claim was resolved by a final order of the bankruptcy court that had jurisdiction over the claims arising out of the common nucleus of facts pertaining to the listing agreement and entitlement to the fees under the agreement. There is nothing on the face of the complaint to suggest that the seller's claims were not disclosed in bankruptcy due to inadvertence or mistake or could not have been properly presented to the bankruptcy court via an objection. See Hamilton v. Greenwich Investors (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1602; Ah Quin v. County of Kauai Dept. of Transp. (9th Cir. 2013) 733 F.3d 267; In re Bevan (2003) 327 F.3d 994; Stern v. Marshall (2011) 564 U.S. 462; and In Re McElwee (2012) 469 B.R. 566, Cross-complainant does not suggest that any amendments may be made in good faith to overcome the issue raised by the demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
